name: Dipyrone
executiveSummary: Analgesic, antipyretic. Acute pain due to trauma or surgery. Colic pain. Cancer pain or other severe acute or chronic pain refractory to other analgesics. Hyperpyrexia resistant to other drugs.
indications:
## Main indications

Analgesic, antipyretic. Acute pain due to trauma or surgery. Colic pain. Cancer pain or other severe acute or chronic pain refractory to other analgesics. Hyperpyrexia resistant to other drugs.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

pediatrico.presentation: Ampoules 2 mL: 500 mg/mL; Tablets: 500 mg; Suspension: 50 mg/mL
pediatrico.dose: 10 mg/kg/dose every 6 h. Adults: 0,5–1 g/dose every 6 h.
pediatrico.notes: PO as third-line drug after paracetamol and ibuprofen. Precautions: < 6 months, when combined with heparin there is risk of bleeding, patients with hepatopathy. Contraindications: porphyria, leukopenia, allergy to pyrazolones, hemorrhagic syndrome.

stability:
## Pediatric guide

- Discard remainder once opened.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Agranulocytosis, aplastic anemia, severe cutaneous reactions, hypotension, bronchospasm, nausea, vomiting, dizziness, headache, diaphoresis, anaphylaxis.
